title: JWT Best Practices: Security, Storage, and Rotation
body: May 2, 2026 · A comprehensive guide to JWT security best practices covering token storage, key rotation, claim validation, refresh token rotation, and Keycloak config.

title: Securely Storing JWTs on the Frontend — and How to Refresh ...

title: JWT in Practice – Part 2: Refresh Tokens, Expiration, and ...
body: Jun 4, 2025 · 1. Token Expiration: Why It Matters JWTs should always have an expiration time (exp claim). A token that never expires is a security risk — if leaked, it grants unlimited access. Recommended Strategy: Short lifespan for access tokens (e.g., 15 minutes) Use a separate refresh token to extend sessions This balances security with user experience.

title: 5 Essential JWT Security Strategies: Rotation and Revocation
body: Nov 2, 2025 · Token rotation means regularly refreshing your access and refresh tokens to limit the lifespan of compromised credentials. Instead of issuing long-lived tokens that stay valid for weeks, you issue short-lived ones and continuously replace them with new ones.
